excluded_defs.name: ReadDeferred
excluded_defs.reason: replaced: no longer called in the non-buffered async path; still called from the sync path

exploration_log.aha_moment: The private `ReadUnbufferedAsync<T>(int index, Func<DbDataReader, object> deserializer, CancellationToken cancel)` already exists and uses `reader.ReadAsync()` and `OnAfterGridAsync`. Since `ValidateAndMarkConsumed` is already called in `ReadAsyncImpl<T>`, I can pass `index` and `deserializer` directly to avoid double-marking.

dead_ends.why_irrelevant: Would double-call ValidateAndMarkConsumed since ReadAsyncImpl already called it; instead, called the inner ReadUnbufferedAsync<T>(index, deserializer, cancel) directly

exploration_log.hindsight: The return type mismatch between IAsyncEnumerable<T> and Task<IEnumerable<T>> means we still buffer; the fix is primarily about using async I/O rather than truly streaming.

alternatives: Throw NotSupportedException for buffered:false and redirect to ReadUnbufferedAsync<T>()
Change return type to IAsyncEnumerable<T> (breaking change)
key_decisions.decision: Buffer the IAsyncEnumerable<T> using await foreach into a List<T> and return as Task<IEnumerable<T>>
key_decisions.reasoning: Preserves the existing public API while switching to truly async I/O (reader.ReadAsync) and OnAfterGridAsync in the non-buffered path. The task says to delegate to ReadUnbufferedAsync<T> which is what we do.

solve_notes: The non-buffered path in `ReadAsyncImpl<T>` called `ReadDeferred<T>` (a sync iterator using `reader.Read()` and `OnAfterGrid`) wrapped in `Task.FromResult`, blocking the thread. The fix delegates to `ReadUnbufferedAsync<T>(index, deserializer, cancel)` (which uses `reader.ReadAsync()` and `OnAfterGridAsync`) via a new `ReadUnbufferedToEnumerableAsync<T>` helper that buffers the results while using truly async I/O.
task_text:
Fix GridReader.ReadAsync<T> with buffered:false executing synchronously

`GridReader.ReadAsync<T>(buffered: false)` in
`SqlMapper.GridReader.Async.cs` returns `Task.FromResult(ReadDeferred<T>(...))`,
wrapping a synchronous iterator in an already-completed task. This
causes `reader.NextResult()` to be called synchronously via
`OnAfterGrid` rather than via the truly-async `OnAfterGridAsync` path,
blocking the calling thread on database I/O. Fix
`SqlMapper.GridReader.Async.cs` so `ReadAsync<T>(buffered: false)`
delegates to `ReadUnbufferedAsync<T>()`, which already uses
`ReadAsync` and `OnAfterGridAsync`.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: ReadDeferred
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: an AI agent would check this to understand the sync iterator being replaced and confirm it calls reader.Read() and OnAfterGrid synchronously

thrash_preventing_defs.name: ReadAsyncUnbufferedImpl
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: an AI agent would check whether to call this method directly (it double-marks) or the inner ReadUnbufferedAsync<T>(index, deserializer, cancel)

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set contains the edited methods. `ReadDeferred` in GridReader.cs is needed by an AI agent to understand exactly what is being replaced (sync reader.Read() and sync OnAfterGrid). `ReadAsyncUnbufferedImpl` is needed to decide whether to call the public delegate or the private inner method (avoid double-marking ValidateAndMarkConsumed). Without these, an agent would search for and read them anyway before implementing.
